name: wrap-happy-path
description: A public wrap (no FHE encryption needed) produces a ConfidentialTransfer log, a token_event row with cleartextAmount, and a decrypted API response.

given.narrative: account[0] holds WETHMock; cWETHMock is the indexed token.

when.narrative: Approve and wrap 1e18 wei of WETHMock into cWETHMock (1 underlying token = 1,000,000 wrapper units).

log.narrative: The wrap tx receipt contains a ConfidentialTransfer log from cWETHMock AND an underlying Transfer(account0 → cWETHMock, 12345) log.

db.narrative: A token_event row with kind='wrap', toAddr=account[0], cleartextAmount=12345.

api.narrative: /v1/accounts/<account0>/transfers includes the wrap with amount=12345, status=decrypted.

name: spiking-throughput
description: Measures decryption throughput under a realistic load spike: N accounts each perform SPIKE_N confidential transfers (generating amount handles), then all simultaneously delegate to the indexer. The decrypt worker drains the handle backlog; throughput = handles/sec from first to last decryption.

given.narrative: SPIKE_ACCOUNTS mnemonic-derived accounts, each funded with ETH (gas) and WETHMock (underlying). INDEXER = mnemonic index 9.

when.narrative: Phase 1: each account wraps underlying into cWETH then sends SPIKE_N confidential transfers to acct[(i+1)%ACCOUNTS]. Phase 2: all accounts delegate to INDEXER near-simultaneously.

then.narrative: Phase 3: poll app.cleartext until >=95% of expected handles are decrypted or timeout. Report throughput = handles / (t_last - t_first) in handles/sec.
